seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Haystack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Haystack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Chatbot with Haystack enables scalable retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines for conversational agents. Guardrails are essential to prevent failures like hallucinations, irrelevant retrievals, and incoherent multi-turn conversations that erode user trust. Vex integration provides runtime verification for response accuracy, safety, and coherence in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Haystack
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Haystack AI Chatbot uses a Pipeline with an Embedder for query encoding, a Retriever (e.g., BM25 or Dense) to fetch relevant documents from a DocumentStore like InMemory or Weaviate, and a PromptBuilder to construct RAG prompts. A Generator (e.g., OpenAIChat) produces responses, while an IntentClassifier handles routing for multi-turn memory via a ConversationMemory component. Custom components manage safety filtering and PII redaction before final output.

Multi-turn context is preserved using session-based memory stores, with intent classification triggering actions like fallback responses or tool calls. The pipeline processes user input sequentially: classify intent → retrieve context → generate → verify → respond, ensuring coherent and grounded conversations.

This modular design allows easy insertion of Vex as a verification component post-generation to check hallucinations, PII leaks, and coherence against retrieved context.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Input
    ↓
IntentClassifier → MemoryLoader
    ↓
PromptBuilder ← Retriever ← DocumentStore
    ↓
Generator
    ↓
[Vex Verifier]
    ↓
SafetyFilter → Response
    ↓
User

risks.name: Retriever returning irrelevant documents
risks.description: BM25 or embedding retriever fetches off-topic docs, leading to hallucinated or inaccurate responses
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks between query, retrieved docs, and generated output

risks.name: Generator hallucinating beyond retrieved context
risks.description: LLM invents facts not grounded in retrieved documents, breaking RAG fidelity
risks.mitigation: Vex hallucination detection via context faithfulness scoring

risks.name: Pipeline component output type mismatches
risks.description: Type errors between components (e.g., wrong Document format) cause silent failures
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation on all component outputs

risks.name: Custom component errors silently swallowed
risks.description: Safety filter or memory component crashes without traceback in production
risks.mitigation: Vex error capture and alerting with full stack traces

implementation.title: Basic RAG Chatbot Pipeline
implementation.description: Core pipeline with retriever, prompt builder, and generator for single-turn Q&A
implementation.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIChatGenerator
from haystack.components.builders import PromptBuilder

retrieval = Pipeline()
retrieval.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever(document_store))
retrieval.add_component("prompt_builder", PromptBuilder(template="Answer using context: {{documents}} Question: {{query}}"))
retrieval.add_component("generator", OpenAIChatGenerator(api_key="your-key"))
retrieval.connect("retriever.documents", "prompt_builder.documents")
retrieval.connect("retriever.query", "prompt_builder.query")
retrieval.connect("prompt_builder", "generator")

implementation.title: Multi-Turn Memory Integration
implementation.description: Add conversation memory for context-aware responses across turns
implementation.code:
from haystack.components.memory.conversation import ConversationMemory
from haystack.components.builders import ChatPromptBuilder

chat_pipeline = Pipeline()
chat_pipeline.add_component("memory", ConversationMemory(session_id="chat-1"))
chat_pipeline.add_component("prompt", ChatPromptBuilder(template="Socratic tutor: {{history}} Current: {{query}}"))
chat_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIChatGenerator())
chat_pipeline.connect("memory.output", "prompt.history")
chat_pipeline.connect("prompt", "generator")
chat_pipeline.connect("generator.replies", "memory.input")

implementation.title: Intent Classification Router
implementation.description: Classify user intent to route between RAG, fallback, or tool calls
implementation.code:
from haystack.components.classifiers import OpenAIDocumentEmbedder
from haystack.utils import ComponentDevice

intent_pipe = Pipeline()
intent_pipe.add_component("embedder", OpenAIDocumentEmbedder(api_key="your-key"))
intents = ["rag_query", "greeting", "fallback"]
# Simplified intent classification
result = intent_pipe.run({"embedder": {"text": user_query}})
if result["embedder"]["embedding"].similarity(intents[0]) > 0.8:
    result = rag_pipeline.run(query=user_query)
else:
    result = fallback_pipeline.run(query=user_query)

implementation.title: Safety and PII Filtering
implementation.description: Custom component to redact PII and filter unsafe content before response

vexIntegration.title: Vex Verifier Custom Component
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as pipeline component to verify response accuracy and hallucination

vexIntegration.title: Insert Vex into RAG Pipeline
vexIntegration.description: Connect VexVerifier after generator for end-to-end verification
vexIntegration.code:
rag_pipeline = Pipeline()
rag_pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever(store))
rag_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIChatGenerator())
rag_pipeline.add_component("vex", VexVerifier())

rag_pipeline.connect("retriever", "generator")
rag_pipeline.connect("generator.response", "vex.response")
rag_pipeline.connect("retriever.query", "vex.query")
rag_pipeline.connect("retriever.documents", "vex.documents")

result = rag_pipeline.run({
    "retriever": {"query": "What is Haystack?"},
    "generator": {"prompt": "Answer using context:"}
})
print(result['vex']['verified_response'])
